_Reference context — observed facts and standing conventions for this project, not instructions. It informs the work; it does not command actions. Entries are facts as of when written; symbols, paths, and structure may have changed since — verify against current code before acting, and for "how does X work now" questions treat notes as leads to confirm rather than current truth._

## Architecture

- The `welpy/` modules layer as an acyclic DAG that stays acyclic with no callback injection between modules because the shared client-lookup queries (`clients_in`/`clients_visible`/`client_monitor`) live in `model` -- so `geometry` is a clean `model`+`layout` leaf -- and `focus` isn't the hub it appears (`focus.reconcile`'s `activate_lock`/`activate_unmanaged` read only `Server` fields). `key_bindings`/`modkey` sit in `app` at the top because they reference `commands` + lifecycle (placing them lower would cycle), so `input` reads the binding table off `server.bindings` and `commands` is driven by `app`, neither importing the other.
- Cross-module deps the linear DAG order hides: `focus` also imports `layout`+`ext_workspace` (`recent_tiled_leaf`/`active_container` use `layout`; `focus.reconcile` calls `ext_workspace.publish`), and `output` also imports `session_lock`+`ext_workspace` (`output.reconcile` drives lock layout + workspace publish).
- IME (native-Wayland input, e.g. fcitx5) lives in `text_input.py`, a `model`-only sink (like `ext_workspace`) relaying the focused `text-input-v3` field to the single `input-method-v2`, both ways; wired from `focus` (`relay_set_focus` at its 3 keyboard-focus seams), `input` (`forward_to_im`/`forward_modifiers_to_im`), and `app.setup`. Ref: sway `input/text_input.c`+`keyboard.c`; dwl has none.
- IME keyboard ownership: while the IME holds the grab, keys + modifiers route to the grab (not the seat) and the IME re-injects pass-through input via its own `virtual-keyboard-v1`. `text_input.im_grab_for(server, group)` gates this, returning None for the IME's own vkb (loop-breaker, keyed on vkb `wl_client` vs. the grab's); bindings dispatch before any forward.
- IME candidate popups parent directly under the focused window's `client.scene_tree` (xdg+X11 only; layer/lock get none, no global popup layer) via `anchor_for_surface` (wired in `app.setup` to `focus.ime_window_anchor`, returning `(scene_tree, origin, output_box)`) -- welpy's simplification of sway's global-layer + `desc.relative` model.
- IME popup placement: `text_input.place_popup` ports sway `constrain_popup` (caret bottom-left, flip up/left on edge overflow). `origin` = the window's surface-buffer top-left in layout coords = `scene_tree` node coords + `model.BORDER_WIDTH − geo.x/y` (welpy puts `content_tree` at `(bw,bw)` with a `(geo)` subsurface clip); surface-local `cursor_rectangle` adds onto it, `_place_node` rebases to parent-tree-local.
- Intra-package imports are relative (`from . import layout`); `welpy/__init__.py` defines the `override`/`_install` hook.
- `tests/__init__.py` exists so `from tests.helpers import ...` resolves under pytest's prepend import mode.
- Reference implementations consulted for behavior parity: dwl, sway, qtile. Source via nix: `nix-build '<nixpkgs>' -A dwl.src` (sway: `-A sway-unwrapped.src`; qtile: `-A python3Packages.qtile.src`).
- Pointer focus pipeline: `forward_pointer_motion(server, time)` re-points the seat pointer to the surface under the cursor; called from the pointer-motion handlers (`cursor_motion`, `cursor_motion_absolute`, and the `cursor_button` release path) and from `focus.reconcile` (re-sample after the scene changes with no mouse move). `surface_at` resolves the surface via `wlr_scene_node_at`.
- `wlr_pointer_constraints_v1_constraint_for_surface(constraints, surface, seat)` resolves the pointer constraint bound to a surface, so the active constraint is looked up from the pointer-focused surface rather than by walking `pointer_constraints->constraints` (dwl walks the list manually; sway uses this helper).
- Pointer motion funnels through one core, `process_pointer_motion(server, device, delta, unaccel, time_msec)`: `cursor_motion` (relative) and `cursor_motion_absolute` (absolute -> layout delta) are thin shims over it that stream the raw delta to relative-pointer clients, enforce the active pointer constraint, then move the cursor + forward the hover (or `drag_client` while grabbed).
- `apply_pointer_constraint(server, dx, dy)` is the pointer-lock override seam: returns the possibly region-confined delta, or `None` when the pointer is locked (caller pins the cursor and skips the focus re-point).
- `server.active_constraint` is `None` or the raw `wlr_pointer_constraint_v1` currently in effect (equal to the `.constraint` of one of the `PointerConstraint` records in `server.constraints`), not a record itself. Invariant: when `server.constraints` is empty, `active_constraint` is `None` -- a record and its active flag clear together in `constraint_destroy` -- so `apply_pointer_constraint` early-returns on empty `server.constraints`, skipping the per-motion `constraint_for_surface` cffi call in the no-lock common case without dropping a pending deactivation.
- Scene hit-testing (`wlr_scene_node_at`) uses the committed surface size clamped by the clip; welpy always sets a clip (`wlr_scene_subsurface_tree_set_clip`), and the clip only *shrinks* the hit region, never grows it. So a window's hittable area lags its configured size until the client commits the new buffer.
- `bump_focus_order` only bumps `client.focus_order`; all focus effects (keyboard focus, raise, border colors, and the pointer re-point) are emitted by `focus.reconcile` at the handler boundary.
- Outside an interactive grab (during a move/resize grab, motion drives `drag_client` instead), welpy re-points pointer focus on every motion — no implicit-grab / dwl-style "CurPressed" focus lock — so pointer focus is not held on a surface across a button press.
- `XdgClient.pending_serial` tracks a compositor-driven configure until the client renders it (the per-client analog of a transaction). Serial tracking and the screen-hold (`client_holds_paint`) are xdg-only; `X11Client` has no configure-serial tracking.
- Window tiling is a per-workspace n-ary tree: `Workspace.root` is a `layout.Container`, and `geometry.reconcile` walks it over `window_area` with equal splits along each container's axis (HORIZONTAL/VERTICAL). It replaced the retired stateless `master_stack`/`zoom`/`cycle_focus`, all now gone.
- `layout.py` holds the pure tree logic: leaves are opaque (a `Container` is told from a leaf by `isinstance` only, never by reading leaf fields, so `layout.py` never imports `Client`), and the handler modules (`commands`/`geometry`/`windows`) are the side-effecting boundary that run the helpers on each `Workspace.root`.
- `server.clients` is a flat registry only; window z-order, focus, and focus-on-close are driven by `focus_order`, a monotonic counter bumped on each focus (higher = more recent).
- `workspace.fullscreen` and per-window `floating_geom` are orthogonal: `client_layer` resolves a window as fullscreen → float → tile, and fullscreen overlays geometry without clearing `floating_geom`, so leaving fullscreen restores the prior float-or-tile state.
- Tree-layout invariant, kept in sync at map/unmap/float/move: a window is a leaf in its `workspace.root` iff `floating_geom is None`. Floats and fullscreen live outside the tree; fullscreen is a render-only override (the window keeps its leaf, and `geometry.reconcile` early-returns after sizing it to the full box, leaving the hidden tiles untouched until exit).
- Tile→float detach is centralized in `float_client(client)` (seed `floating_geom` from the current outer rect, then `layout.remove` the leaf); called by `toggle_floating` and mouse move/resize start (`begin_dragging_client`/`begin_resizing_client`). Route new tile→float entry points through it — setting `floating_geom` on a tiled window without removing its leaf strands a ghost leaf and breaks the leaf-iff-`floating_geom is None` invariant (a fixed bug). Its `workspace is not None` guard covers orphaned tiled windows (no tree to remove from).
- `server.bindings` is a flat `{(modifier_mask, code): action(server)}` dict built by `key_bindings` (user-extensible via `welpy.override`); keyboard keycodes and mouse-button codes share the one dict.
- Both `keyboard_key` (press only, while unlocked) and `cursor_button` (mouse buttons) resolve a press through `lookup_binding(server, mods, code)` -- the action-or-None seam (None forwards the input to the focused app), defined below both callers per the "callees below callers" order. It is the documented override point for modal submaps.
- Keybinding dispatch is edge-triggered -- a held key fires once, no auto-repeat -- and raw media keys carry no modifier (mods=0), dispatching the same way.
- `server.passthrough` (toggled by `toggle_passthrough`, bound to mod+shift+p) makes `lookup_binding` return None for every action except `toggle_passthrough` itself, forwarding all input to the focused app; the still-live toggle is the escape hatch. Built for nested/dev sessions.
- `build_keycode_map` resolves xkb **sym names** -> evdev keycodes once (from the default keymap `xkb_keymap_new_from_names(ctx, NULL, 0)`, layout 0/level 0 to strip Shift) so bindings can key off names. That keymap already includes XF86 media keys, so `server.keycode["XF86AudioMute"]`/`LowerVolume`/`RaiseVolume` (evdev 113/114/115) resolve with no special handling -- their presence depends on system xkb data, not welpy source (verified via `xkbcli compile-keymap`).
- A window's current size is cached in `Client.inner_size` -- the inner/content `(width, height)` tuple (its box minus the border on each side; `None` before first map), set by `resize_client` each layout pass. Readers use it to get a window's size directly instead of re-deriving geometry via `layout.walk`: clip sizing (`apply_clip`), configure dedup (`set_size`), X11 reconfigure (`xwayland.on_request_configure`/`drag_client`), and `group_window`'s long-side test (where the symmetric border cancels in the width-vs-height comparison).
- `keyboard_key`/`keyboard_modifiers` take `group=None` (defaults to physical `server.keyboard_group`) and `wlr_seat_set_keyboard` to the emitting group's keyboard before forwarding, so the focused app (and key repeat) reads that keyboard's keymap.
- Each `virtual-keyboard-unstable-v1` keyboard gets its own isolated `wlr_keyboard_group`: `input.virtual_keyboard_new` creates a `model.VirtualKeyboard`, tracks it in `server.virtual_keyboards`, and `virtual_keyboard_destroy` tears it down.
- `KeyboardGroup` is physical-keyboard state only and owns `keymap`/`xkb_context`; virtual keyboards use `VirtualKeyboard` so `destroy_keyboard_group` never sees client-owned keymaps.
- `bindings.wl_list_for_each(ffi, head, ctype, member)` is a generic intrusive `wl_list` iterator (the libwayland container_of idiom), logic-in-Python with no bespoke C helper. `client_rendered` is its only caller, walking a surface's `current_outputs` with `any(...)` to test on-screen presence.

## Conventions

- Multiline parenthesized expressions put the opening `(` alone on its line, operands indented one level, and the closing `)` dedented on its own line (not trailing the last operand). **Why:** the chosen readability style here; existing code is mixed, so it isn't reliably inferable from a sample. **How to apply:** any parenthesized expression spanning lines, e.g. a conditional/ternary assignment -- the `axis = (...)` ternary in `layout.move` (and `commands.group_window`) is a worked example; note `layout.cycle` predates the rule and still trails its closing paren.
- `layout.py` docstrings describe tree operations only; UI keybindings (`mod+v` etc.) are named in command/handler docstrings and `app.key_bindings`, never in the pure layout module. **Why:** key mentions in `wrap`/`unwrap`/`cycle` silently went stale when bindings were rebound (group `mod+w`->`mod+v`, axis-flip->`mod+e`); the pure module has no reason to track UI and naming keys there only invites rot. **How to apply:** when adding or renaming a layout helper, don't cite a key in its docstring.
- Standalone planning/spec/review docs and the session transcript (`session.jsonl`) are throwaway working artifacts, removed from the repo once work lands; durable design rationale lives in code comments and `MEMORY.md`. **Why:** keeps the tree to source + tests and avoids stale design docs. **How to apply:** don't expect a committed plan/spec for a feature; delete such files (and the session log) with the landing commit.
- `pytest` and `pylint` are pinned in the project `shell.nix` (alongside `deps.nix`), so they run via plain `nix-shell --run "pytest"` / `nix-shell --run "pylint ."`, never `nix-shell --packages ...`. **Why:** `--packages python3Packages.pytest` skips the project's pinned deps and can diverge from the real toolchain. **How to apply:** run either through `nix-shell --run`. The suite mocks the wlroots C bindings (no real backend/socket/subprocess), so both are read-only and non-destructive.
- pylint `duplicate-code` (R0801) on shared test-setup boilerplate across the per-module test files is suppressed with an inline `# pylint: disable=duplicate-code` at each flagged spot, never a global `.pylintrc` disable. **Why:** a global disable also blinds genuine duplication in `welpy/` source, and per-file/path scoping is unreliable for R0801 -- pylint attributes a cross-file cluster to a single (sometimes a third) file, and a module-level disable placed wrong (e.g. inside the module docstring) silently no-ops. **How to apply:** when duplicated fixture/setup blocks trip R0801, disable it inline at the test functions involved.
- Pure-Python binding helpers that take an `ffi` (e.g. `bindings.wl_list_for_each`) are unit-tested with a throwaway **ABI-mode** `cffi.FFI()` -- a plain `ffi.cdef(...)` + `ffi.new(...)`, no `set_source`/compile and no wlroots headers. **Why:** the suite's `ffi`/`lib` are `MagicMock`s that can't do real `offsetof`/`addressof`/pointer arithmetic, so the container_of logic would otherwise go unexercised (or hang on a mock `while node != head`). **How to apply:** to test an ffi-taking helper's logic, build a real FFI with a minimal struct cdef; mock only the wlroots-specific surface so higher-level callers (`client_rendered`) still stub via `patch`.
- To assert cffi **out-param** math under the mock `lib`/`ffi` harness (e.g. confine/cursor-hint deltas), set `server.ffi.new.side_effect = real.new` from a throwaway `cffi.FFI()` so `ffi.new("double *")[0]` round-trips, then give the C-helper mock a `side_effect` that writes concrete values into the out-pointers. **Why:** MagicMock cdata doesn't remember `[0]` writes, so the delta arithmetic would otherwise be mock-on-mock and untestable. **How to apply:** only `ffi.new` needs to be real; `ffi.cast`/`addressof`/`NULL` stay mocked.
- Tests patch by the canonical dotted string (`patch("welpy.app.X")` / `patch("welpy.<mod>.X")`) and reference symbols by their real home (`app.setup`, `model.Layer`, `layout.Rect`). **Why:** a `patch(...)` target *string* is a module path, not an attribute ref, so it must name the symbol's owning module. **How to apply:** patch the real dotted path; import each symbol from its owning module.
- `welpy.override` `setattr`s the hook's home module, so a cross-module qualified call (`focus.reconcile`) or a same-module bare call sees the override, but `from .focus import reconcile` elsewhere silently binds the pre-override original (no error). Types are imported by-name from their home (`from .model import Server`); tests do the same (`model.Layer`, `layout.Rect`). The qualify-extension-points rule (incl. tunable constants) is in `AGENTS.md`.
- welpy's leaf modules are dependency **sinks**: `layout`/`ext_workspace`/`libinput` never import the compositor; `ext_workspace` inverts the edge via injected callbacks (`on_activate`/`on_assign`) rather than calling back in. **Why:** keeps the import graph acyclic (a pylint `cyclic-import` gate). **How to apply:** a new module needing core behavior takes a callback or stays a pure leaf, never importing `app`.

## Gotchas

- wlroots 0.20 emits the `wlr_input_method_v2` `commit`/`destroy` and keyboard-grab `destroy` signals with **NULL** listener `data` (only the `new_*`/`grab_keyboard` signals pass an object; the header's `// struct ...` is a type hint, not the data). Read the object from the relay, never `ffi.cast(..., data)` -- that derefs NULL and segfaults on focus change (the IME drops its grab on focus loss). sway's `data`-based handlers target older wlroots.
- Forwarding only keys (not modifiers) to the IME grab loses Ctrl/Alt chords -- the IME, unaware a modifier is held, passes Ctrl-U through as bare `u`; `keyboard_modifiers` must send modifiers to the grab too (XOR seat), like sway's `handle_modifier_event`.
- An IME popup's subtree is a child of the focused window's `client.scene_tree`, which wlroots destroys recursively on window unmap; the relay watches the anchored surface's unmap and nulls `popup.scene_tree` **without destroying the node** (the window tree frees it) -- order-independent vs. `windows.on_unmap` since the handler never derefs it. Re-focus recreates the subtree.
- IME-on-lockscreen asymmetry: while `server.locked`, `keyboard_key`'s top guard drops all vkb keys, so an IME's pass-through keys are silently dropped at the locker -- though physical keys reach the grab and `commit_string` still reaches the lock surface. Not a "fcitx broken on lockscreen" bug.
- Fullscreen/resize pointer landmine: re-pointing pointer focus runs synchronously (e.g. `toggle_fullscreen` → `focus.reconcile`) *before* the client commits its new-size buffer. The cursor is hit-tested against the old (already repositioned) buffer, so if it falls outside, focus is cleared and nothing re-samples it until the next input — scroll and the first click are lost. Mitigated by `rebase_pointer` at the input dispatch sites.
- cffi pointer equality: `==` compares by address and is safe against `None` (`ffi.NULL == None` is False; a non-null cdata `== None` is False). `rebase_pointer`'s dedup relies on this.
- `ffi.addressof(cdata, "field")` requires `cdata` to be a struct *value*, not a pointer; to take the address of a field reachable through a pointer `p`, dereference first -- `ffi.addressof(p[0], "field")`. `client_rendered` uses `ffi.addressof(surface[0], "current_outputs")` for exactly this. Partial structs declared with `...` still resolve `ffi.offsetof`/`addressof` (offsets come from the real headers at compile).
- Compiling against `wlr_pointer_constraints_v1.h` needs the generated `<wayland-protocols/pointer-constraints-unstable-v1-enum.h>`, which wlroots does **not** install -- generate it with `builder.enum_header("wayland-protocols", "unstable/pointer-constraints/pointer-constraints-unstable-v1.xml", "pointer-constraints-unstable-v1")`, the xdg-shell pattern. The relative-pointer header includes only `wayland-server-core.h`, so it needs none; `wlr_virtual_keyboard_v1.h` (only `wayland-server-core.h` + `wlr_keyboard.h`) likewise needs no scanner/enum header. `wlr_text_input_v3.h`/`wlr_input_method_v2.h` need neither either (no `-enum.h` include, send-code in libwlroots, `WLR_TEXT_INPUT_V3_FEATURE_*` resolve as `#define`).
- `scripts/gdb-welpy` runs welpy nested under gdb for native cffi/wlroots segfaults, logging to `gdb-welpy.log`. gdb isn't in `shell.nix`; the script adds it via `nix-shell -E 'mkShell { inputsFrom = [ (import ./shell.nix {}) ]; packages = [ gdb ]; }'` -- the pattern for layering a tool onto the pinned project env.
- A `wlr_keyboard_group` copies any member's keymap onto its siblings and itself (`handle_keyboard_keymap`); a virtual keyboard uploads its own keymap, so sharing a group with the physical keyboard overwrites -- and outlives -- the real keymap, bricking it on client exit (fixed: isolated group per vkb).
- `input.virtual_keyboard_new` adds its keyboard-destroy listener AFTER `wlr_keyboard_group_add_keyboard`, so wlroots' member-destroy (registered inside add_keyboard) runs first; `virtual_keyboard_destroy` then frees an emptied group. Reordering risks a use-after-free.
- `wlr_pointer_constraint_v1_send_deactivated()` may synchronously destroy the constraint (oneshot lifetime), firing its `destroy` listener mid-call -- a constraint activate/deactivate transition must tolerate its own active reference being cleared during the deactivate.
- `_path(root, leaf)` returns `(container, index)` pairs, yet `_collapse(path)` re-derives each container's index in its parent live via `_index` instead of reusing the carried index. **Why:** `move` inserts the relocated leaf into a path container *before* calling `_collapse`, which shifts later positions, so a stored index would drop/promote the wrong child; `remove` has no pre-collapse insert and would be safe, but `_collapse` is shared by both. Don't "optimize" `_collapse` to trust the path's indices.
- `layout.move`'s descend/pop-out path detaches the leaf from its source parent *before* inserting it under the receiving ancestor, so that ancestor is never momentarily single-child/empty and survives the trailing `_collapse`. Inserting before detaching would let `_collapse` drop or promote the wrong node.
- `Client` (`@dataclass(kw_only=True)`) and `layout.Container` (`@dataclass`) both default to `eq=True, frozen=False`, so instances are **unhashable** (`__hash__` is None) and `==` compares by field value. `layout.py` consequently operates on tree leaves by identity only: it never hashes a leaf (no leaf-keyed `dict`/`set`; `leaves`/`adjacent_leaves` build lists, never `dict(walk(...))`) and never leans on `==` (`_index`/`_path`/`container_of` use `is`). A future leaf-keyed dict or `list.remove(leaf)` would raise "unhashable type" or drop the wrong node. `make_client()` builds real `XdgClient` dataclasses, not mocks, so tests hit the same constraint.
- Passthrough (and any future submap) exempts its own toggle by **identity** (`action is not toggle_passthrough`), so that binding must store the bare function, not a lambda wrapping it -- a lambda breaks the exemption and traps input.
- Adding a built-in keybinding that references a new key name fails ~23 `setup`/`make_server` tests with `KeyError` until the name is added to `make_keycode_map` in `tests/helpers.py` (it lists only keys used by built-in bindings); a new `Server` field likewise needs a default added to `make_server` (also in `tests/helpers.py`).
- pylint's `line-too-long` gate is 80 cols (`.pylintrc`), but it exempts any physical line carrying a trailing `# pylint:` pragma, so some 100+ char lines stay unflagged -- an over-long line isn't necessarily a new regression.
- `geometry.configure_x11` carries no leading underscore despite being a low-level helper, because `xwayland`/`input` call it cross-module and a leading `_` trips pylint `protected-access` (W0212) at the caller.
- A module-level `from welpy import <mod>` / `from . import <mod>` shadows any local of the same name -- `patch(...) as <mod>:` mock vars, plain assignments, or a `setup()` C-server local. pylint flags W0621, and code that also calls the real `<mod>.fn(...)` hits `UnboundLocalError`; the convention is to rename the local (mock vars to the patched symbol e.g. `as focus_client`, source C-server locals to `<mod>_server`), leaving the module's `<mod>.X` calls intact.
- A `welpy/` submodule named after a Python builtin -- `welpy/input.py` -- makes `from . import input` (and `from welpy import (... input)`) trip pylint W0622 redefined-builtin; every such import line carries a `# pylint: disable=redefined-builtin` pragma (in `app.py`, `tests/test_input.py`, and `tests/test_app.py`). The builtin is unused in this codebase, so the shadow is harmless.
- `welpy.override`'s `__qualname__`-walk only resolves a target whose dotted qualname maps to real module attributes: a real method or a prior `override` wrapper (which keeps the dotted qualname) works, but a target whose attribute is a bare lambda (qualname `...<lambda>`) or a method of a function-local class (qualname contains `<locals>`) raises `AttributeError` on the walk. Tests must patch the base with a function carrying the right home + dotted qualname, or override the real method and restore in `finally`. Also: `override` now reads `target.__qualname__` (not `__name__`) up front, so a non-introspectable callable (e.g. `functools.partial`) raises `AttributeError: __qualname__`.

## Decisions

- `text_input.py` is a `model`-only sink so `focus` calls `relay_set_focus` directly (like `ext_workspace.publish`); behavior needing `focus`/`geometry` is inverted via an injected `anchor_for_surface` callback rather than a back-edge. Rejected placing `text_input` after `focus` with a stored `Server` callback for set_focus -- extra indirection on the common path, diverges from the `ext_workspace` precedent.
- `anchor_for_surface` returns `(scene_tree, origin, output_box)` -- it carries the `scene_tree` (not just geometry) because the `text_input` sink can't resolve surface→window itself, and the popup parents under that tree.
- Stale-pointer-after-fullscreen fix is a dispatch-site rebase (`rebase_pointer`) in `cursor_button` (before `notify_button`) and `cursor_axis` (before `notify_axis`, skipped while a window is grabbed), not a commit-time rebase. Rationale: dispatch-site covers X11 too (no `pending_serial` dependency), fixes both symptoms, and dedups (no-op when focus already matches) to avoid spurious motion events. Accepted trade-off: hover / cursor-image isn't refreshed until the next click or scroll.
- Cross-compositor reference for input-focus work: dwl re-points pointer focus synchronously (e.g. in `arrange()`/`focusclient()` via `motionnotify(0,...)`), never on the resize-completing commit, so it shares the same latent fullscreen ordering bug; sway rebases (`cursor_rebase_all()`) after a transaction applies, once all resized buffers land.
- Pointer-lock (`wlr_pointer_constraints_v1` + `wlr_relative_pointer_v1`) unifies `cursor_motion`/`cursor_motion_absolute` into one `process_pointer_motion` core (dwl `motionnotify` shape). Rejected: surgical per-handler insertion (keeps the two handlers' duplication) and a lock-only MVP.
- Pointer-lock scope is full (relative + locked + confined); confinement uses `wlr_region_confine`, available because `pixman-1` is already in bindings `_PKGS`.
- Pointer-lock constraint tracking follows dwl/sway: a destroy listener per constraint registered at `new_constraint`, plus one `active_constraint` resolved by pointer focus. An "active-only" single listener was rejected because `send_deactivated` can destroy a constraint and inactive constraints still need their own destroy cleanup.
- Pointer-lock ships no unlock keybinding: keybindings keep firing while the pointer is locked (`keyboard_key` dispatches bindings before forwarding) and a constraint is active only while its surface holds pointer focus, so any keyboard-driven focus change (workspace switch, close, focus-other) deactivates it -- that focus-tied deactivation is the escape, matching dwl/sway (neither ships one). Reversed an initial instinct to add an explicit escape hatch.
- Pointer-lock honors the client cursor-hint: on destroy of the *active* constraint (not on focus-loss deactivation), warp the cursor to the hint so it reappears where the app expects (dwl `cursorwarptohint`); surface-local hint -> layout via the client's `content_tree.node` origin (approximate for non-trivial xdg geometry offsets, acceptable for a hint).
- Pointer-lock fully unifies the absolute path: `cursor_motion_absolute` converts to a layout delta + `wlr_cursor_move` (not `wlr_cursor_warp_absolute`) so a lock holds regardless of input source and nested sessions carry constraints (dwl's model). Reactive-only fallback if touch/tablet mapping regresses: keep `warp_absolute` and route only relative motion through constraints.
- Tree layout uses an n-ary container tree (sway/i3 model). Binary BSP was rejected because tabbed/stacked containers (wanted "maybe later") are inherently n-ary and binary needs rebalancing for equal N-way splits; stateless spiral/fibonacci layouts were rejected as not interactively manipulable.
- `ContainerLayout` is orientation-only (`HORIZONTAL`/`VERTICAL`); tabbed/stacked were deliberately kept out of the enum as a separate future concept, not v1.
- Grouping is two keys (replacing the earlier `mod+w` create/destroy toggle): `mod+v` (`group_window`) wraps the focused window alone in a new container split along its long side -- a **no-op when the window has no siblings**, so it never pointlessly nests a lone window and can't double-wrap; `mod+e` (`cycle_layout`) flips a container's axis. There is deliberately **no explicit ungroup** -- groups dissolve only when collapse empties a container or drops it to one child.
- `layout.unwrap` has no production caller: the retired `mod+w` toggle's destroy branch was its only one, and the no-explicit-ungroup decision left nothing to replace it. It survives only as the tested `wrap`/`unwrap` pair.
- Directional focus (`mod+hjkl` -> `focus_direction`) is **structural**, sharing `move`'s `_neighbor` climb: it ascends to the nearest ancestor that splits on the move axis with a sibling that way, then focuses that sibling's **most-recently-focused** window (max `focus_order`). `layout.adjacent_leaves` returns the sibling's windows and `focus_direction` applies the `focus_order` policy, keeping `layout` free of `Client`. No-op at an edge -- unlike `move`, focus has no parent-escape, nothing to land on. Rationale: symmetry with `move` and a "return to where you were" landing.
- Focus-on-close lands on the **lineage-local MRU**: closing a tiled window focuses (via `layout.successor`) the most-recently-focused window in the nearest enclosing container that still holds another -- not the workspace-global MRU -- to keep focus where the user was working. A closed float, or a tile whose tree empties, falls back to `top_client`. Rejected: pure global MRU (jumps to a recently-touched window elsewhere) and a per-container focus stack (deferred until tabbed/stacked exist).
- `layout.successor(root, leaf, select)` both climbs the lineage and returns the single max-by-`select` window, deliberately unlike the `adjacent_leaves`/`focus_direction` split (layout returns candidates, the caller applies the `focus_order` policy): the close path wants one window, and passing `select` (`lambda c: c.focus_order`) keeps `layout` free of `Client`. Intentional -- don't "restore" a candidate-list shape.
- Tree traversal is search-on-demand (DFS from the workspace root) rather than parent back-pointers: trees are tiny (one workspace's windows), so O(n) ascent is cheap and there are no back-refs to keep in sync.
- Tree-layout collapse is the shared `_collapse(path)` helper, run by both `remove` and `move`: it tidies **only the affected leaf's ancestor chain** (drop emptied containers, promote single-child ones), never a global single-child flatten, and never the root. Rationale: a global pass would dissolve an unrelated one-window `mod+v` group on any close; localized leaves it intact. Empty/1-child cleanup is mandatory regardless because a `mod+v` wrap can leave a 1-child container that later empties.
- Directional move (`mod+shift+hjkl` -> `move_direction` -> pure `layout.move`) is a **structural relocate**, sway-style -- chosen over geometric swap (can't pop into open space or restructure nesting) and i3 reorder-only (can't descend into a sibling container). It climbs from the leaf's parent to the first ancestor whose split axis matches the move with a neighbor on the travel side, then reorders past a leaf neighbor, descends into a container neighbor (perpendicular -> index 0, matching-axis -> facing end), or -- at the edge of every matching-axis ancestor -- pops the leaf one level up toward the move side; then `_collapse` tidies the drained source. Only the focused leaf moves; landing is purely structural (no geometry). The sole no-op is a leaf directly under the root with no same-axis sibling on the travel side -- nothing to reorder past, and nothing above the root to escape into (no implicit root split); a root child *with* a same-axis neighbor still reorders.
- `client_holds_paint`'s screen-hold predicate is `client_rendered` (surface on >=1 output) AND `!= Layer.FLOAT`, replacing the coarse `Layer.TILE and workspace.fullscreen is None` proxy. **Why:** fullscreen entry becomes atomic -- the window holds the screen through the transition until its full-size buffer lands, no one-frame flash of the old size. Provably freeze-free: wlroots gates `wlr_scene_buffer_send_frame_done` and `current_outputs` tracking on the same occlusion-aware `node.visible` region, so a window holds iff it's visible iff it will ack -- an occluded peer (the b766954 freeze) can't hold by construction. The `!= Layer.FLOAT` opt-out keeps interactive resize of a dragged float responsive. Rejected: special-casing only the fullscreen window in the coarse proxy; a sway-style deferred per-window transaction on configure-ack. Tests stub `output.client_rendered` since the predicate reads scene state.
- The binding seam is action-level (`lookup_binding(server, mods, code) -> action | None`), chosen over a table-returning `active_bindings(server) -> Mapping`: config can then express "swallow unbound keys in a submap" as a one-line inline default (`table.get(key, lambda s: None)`), where a table hook would need a custom mapping class.
- Modal submaps (sway-style modes / prefix keys) are deliberately kept out of core -- only the `lookup_binding` seam is provided, and the mechanism (state, enter/leave, swallow, escape) lives in user config via a `welpy.override` of `lookup_binding`. Passthrough is the one built-in built on the seam.
- Passthrough is full: because `lookup_binding` is shared with `cursor_button`, it suppresses mouse-button bindings (mod+drag/resize) too, not just keys -- an accepted consequence of the shared seam, not keyboard-only.
- Intended (unbuilt) submap recipe: `enter_submap(server, table, exit_code)` with a **required** exit_code that injects `(0, exit_code) -> leave_submap` so a submap can't trap the keyboard; sticky by default (an action opts into one-shot by calling leave itself); submap keys bound mod-less `(0, code)` with the user releasing the entry modifier; unbound keys swallowed. Key-repeat (needs a timer) and clear-on-lock were deferred.
- `welpy.override` is target-only (`@welpy.override(module.hook)`): it resolves `target.__module__` to the home module, walks `target.__qualname__` from there to find the install owner (bare name -> the module; dotted e.g. `Builder.compile` -> the class), and chains by currying the previous version + rewriting `wrapper.__module__`/`__qualname__`. **Why target rather than by-name:** a by-name form silently no-ops when a hook's home module differs from where a config names it.
- `welpy.override` supports both module-level functions and class methods via one `__qualname__`-walk path (module-level qualname == name, so it's the empty-walk special case); the curry contract for a method is `(prev, self, *args)`. Decided to generalize rather than require every patchable method to delegate to a module-level hook.
- `virtual-keyboard-unstable-v1` uses an isolated `wlr_keyboard_group` per virtual keyboard (dwl-style); dispatch re-points the seat to the emitting keyboard so the client's keymap is respected. Rejected: a shared physical group (bricks the real keymap) and forcing the compositor keymap (mistypes `wtype`'s own).
- Virtual-keyboard events are dropped while `server.locked` by checking `isinstance(group, VirtualKeyboard)`, so a background client can't type into the locker; physical keys (`group is None`) still reach the lock surface.
- Cross-compositor virtual-keyboard auth differs by threat model: dwl accepts every client and lets injected keys hit compositor bindings; sway marks the global privileged and hides it only from `security-context-v1` sandboxed clients, leaving unsandboxed clients trusted.
- Virtual keyboard records are `model.VirtualKeyboard` instead of overloaded `KeyboardGroup` records because the physical group owns xkb resources and virtual records only need group/client/listeners; `eq=False` plus identity removal avoids dataclass value-equality deleting the wrong record.
- The compositor launches via `python -m welpy` (`welpy/__main__.py` -> `welpy.app.main`); the package's canonical identity lets a user config import `welpy` and its submodules directly, with no `sys.modules` aliasing.

## Open Questions

- ? Virtual-keyboard authorization policy is unsettled: matching dwl means current all-client trust, matching sway means a `security-context-v1` global filter, and a default-deny hook would be stricter than both.

## Dead Ends

- ✗ Commit-time rebase (abandoned): re-point the pointer in `windows.on_commit` when the `pending_serial` catch-up fires (sway's transaction-apply model). Dropped — xdg-only (misses X11 fullscreen) and a second mechanism overlapping the dispatch-site rebase.
- ✗ Bare `head.next != head` emptiness test for `client_rendered` (rejected): the generic `bindings.wl_list_for_each` iterator was chosen instead for reuse, so `client_rendered`'s `any(...)` over `current_outputs` is deliberate -- don't "optimize" it back to an inline emptiness check. The path is rarely hit anyway, gated behind an unacked resize.
- ✗ Geometric directional focus (reversed in favor of the structural `_neighbor` climb): the earlier center-to-center "nearest leaf beyond the focused center" rule and its `_center`/`_beyond` helpers were removed; i3's overlap tie-break and cross-monitor edge-jump had already been dropped before that. Geometry can't disambiguate co-located leaves once tabbed/stacked containers share one rect (`walk` gives identical centers).
- ✗ Moving `ffi`/`lib` (and the `listen`/`add_timer`/`add_signal` registerers) off `Server` fields into module-global scope in `bindings` (abandoned): explored only to drop the per-function `ffi, lib = server.ffi, server.lib` idiom -- a cosmetic gain whose dominant cost (migrating every test reference off `server.lib`/`server.ffi` + an autouse conftest patching the globals) is **identical across every read-syntax variant**, so the user dropped it. Source change is small (default `None`, `build()` does `global ffi, lib; ffi, lib = ...`, `event_loop` stays a param so the registerers are process-global-clean); the test churn is the whole cost.
- ✗ `from bindings import ffi, lib` (by-name) for module-global bindings (rejected): a by-name import snapshots the value at import time, but the mocked suite must skip the real compile (globals are `None` at import) then rebind a per-test mock after import -- a snapshot can't observe that rebind. So `ffi`/`lib` must be read **qualified** (`bindings.lib`) or snapshot into a call-time local; by-name works only for the stable defs (`listen`/`add_timer`/`add_signal`/`build`/`wl_list_for_each`). Import-time `build()` (e.g. `if BUILD_EXTENSION: build()` at module bottom) would compile the real wlroots extension on every `pytest` run, gutting the hermetic mocked suite -- the suite's whole point is never compiling.
- ✗ Proxy / callable indirection to enable bare `lib`/`ffi` despite the above (rejected): a stable `_Proxy` forwarding `__getattr__` to a swappable `_target`, or a callable `lib()` returning `lib._value`, both restore by-name + per-test mocking -- but add a custom indirection (proxy pays a per-attribute `__getattr__` tax on the hot path; callable makes `ffi`/`lib` callables-named-as-nouns with a bespoke `._value` mock convention) to save one `bindings.` prefix that *is itself* the seam tests patch. Stock qualified `bindings.lib` + `monkeypatch.setattr` is zero-overhead and self-evident; the callable beats the proxy only if bare reads are a hard requirement.
- ✗ A dedicated `welpy/config.py` for tunable constants (rejected): each constant lives in its owning module, read qualified so configs can rebind it; a separate config module would only add load-ordering complexity (a config runs at startup before the compositor is built) for no gain.

"""Window lifecycle for xdg-shell apps: creating, mapping, unmapping, and
destroying windows, handling their fullscreen/maximize/activation requests,
and placing their transient popups (menus, tooltips)."""

from __future__ import annotations

from . import ext_workspace
from . import focus
from . import geometry
from . import layout
from . import model
from .model import Client, Layer, Server, X11Client, XdgClient


def on_create(server: Server, data) -> None:
    """Fires when an app creates a new window. Attaches lifecycle
    listeners; the scene tree, borders, and layout entry are added at map
    time so creation lands in a single frame."""
    ffi, lib, listen = server.ffi, server.lib, server.listen
    toplevel = ffi.cast("struct wlr_xdg_toplevel *", data)
    client = XdgClient(
        toplevel=toplevel, scene_tree=None, content_tree=None, borders=(),
        focus_order=0, urgent=False, grab=None, floating_geom=None,
        workspace=None, listeners=[], pending_serial=None,
        decoration=None, handle=None, inner_size=None)
    # Back-pointer toplevel -> Client, so per-surface protocols (xdg-decoration
    # etc.) can resolve the owning client before it joins server.clients at map.
    client.handle = ffi.new_handle(client)
    toplevel.base.data = client.handle
    client.listeners.extend([
        listen(lib.welpy_surface_commit(toplevel.base.surface),
            lambda data: on_commit(server, client, data)),
        listen(lib.welpy_surface_map(toplevel.base.surface),
            lambda data: on_map(server, client, data)),
        listen(lib.welpy_surface_unmap(toplevel.base.surface),
            lambda data: on_unmap(server, client, data)),
        listen(lib.welpy_xdg_toplevel_destroy(toplevel),
            lambda data: on_destroy(server, client, data)),
        listen(lib.welpy_xdg_toplevel_request_fullscreen(toplevel),
            lambda data: on_request_fullscreen(server, client, data)),
        listen(lib.welpy_xdg_toplevel_request_maximize(toplevel),
            lambda data: on_request_maximize(server, client, data)),
    ])


def on_commit(server: Server, client: Client, _data) -> None:
    """Fires every time the app commits new state for its window."""
    if isinstance(client, XdgClient):
        if client.toplevel.base.initial_commit:
            # Empty configure; real tile size is sent from on_map.
            geometry.set_size(server, client, 0, 0)
            return
        if client.pending_serial is not None:
            # Release the screen hold once the client has caught up.
            acked = client.toplevel.base.current.configure_serial
            if acked >= client.pending_serial:
                client.pending_serial = None
    # Commits can still arrive after unmap, once the clipped tree is gone.
    if client.scene_tree is not None and client.inner_size is not None:
        # geometry offset can shift between commits (CSD on/off); resync.
        geometry.apply_clip(server, client)


def on_map(server: Server, client: Client, _data) -> None:
    """Fires the first time the window has a buffer to show. Builds the
    window's scene tree, joins the layout, and shifts focus -- all in one
    event so the new window, sibling reflow, and focus highlight land in
    a single frame."""
    lib = server.lib
    create_scene(server, client)
    if server.active_monitor is not None:
        client.workspace = server.active_monitor.active_workspace
    monitor = model.client_monitor(client)
    workspace = client.workspace
    # Un-fullscreen any window already fullscreen on this workspace so the
    # new window isn't buried under it.
    if workspace is not None and workspace.fullscreen is not None:
        geometry.set_fullscreen(server, workspace, None)
    # A new tiled window joins next to the focused one.
    target = focus.top_client(server, monitor)
    server.clients.insert(0, client)
    if geometry.client_wants_float(client) and monitor is not None:
        client.floating_geom = geometry.init_floating_geom(client)
    elif workspace is not None:
        layout.insert_sibling(workspace.root, target, client)
    geometry.set_tiled(
        server, client,
        lib.WLR_EDGE_TOP | lib.WLR_EDGE_BOTTOM
        | lib.WLR_EDGE_LEFT | lib.WLR_EDGE_RIGHT)
    if geometry.client_wants_fullscreen(client) and workspace is not None:
        # Honor a pre-map or initial-commit fullscreen request.
        geometry.set_fullscreen(server, workspace, client)
    focus.bump_focus_order(server, client)
    geometry.apply_hierarchy(server)
    geometry.apply_visibility(server)
    geometry.apply_tree(server)
    if monitor is not None:
        geometry.reconcile(server, monitor)
    focus.reconcile(server)
    # The decoration request may have arrived before the initial configure;
    # now that the surface is initialized, set_mode is safe.
    geometry.apply_decoration(server)


def on_unmap(server: Server, client: Client, _data) -> None:
    """Fires when a window stops showing (close or voluntary hide). Tears
    down the window's scene tree, leaves the layout, reflows siblings,
    and shifts focus to a window beside the closed one -- in one event so
    removal lands in a single frame."""
    ffi, lib = server.ffi, server.lib
    client.grab = None
    monitor = model.client_monitor(client)
    successor = None
    if client.workspace is not None and client.floating_geom is None:
        # Pick the successor before remove(): _collapse drops the lineage.
        successor = layout.successor(
            client.workspace.root, client, lambda c: c.focus_order)
        layout.remove(client.workspace.root, client)
    server.clients.remove(client)
    focus.drop_ime_popups_for_scene_tree(server, client.scene_tree)
    # Wrapper isn't tied to the content role's lifetime
    lib.wlr_scene_node_destroy(ffi.addressof(client.scene_tree.node))
    if isinstance(client, XdgClient):
        client.toplevel.base.surface.data = ffi.NULL
    client.scene_tree = None
    client.content_tree = None
    client.borders = ()
    client.workspace = None
    geometry.apply_hierarchy(server)
    geometry.apply_visibility(server)
    if successor is None:
        successor = focus.top_client(server, monitor)
    if successor is not None:
        focus.bump_focus_order(server, successor)
    if monitor is not None and monitor in server.monitors:
        geometry.reconcile(server, monitor)
    focus.reconcile(server)


def on_request_fullscreen(
        server: Server, client: Client, _data) -> None:
    """An app asked to enter or leave fullscreen."""
    # Pre-map: on_map reads the same flag once the tree exists.
    workspace = client.workspace if client.scene_tree is not None else None
    monitor = (model.client_monitor(client)
               if client.scene_tree is not None else None)
    if workspace is not None and monitor is not None:
        wants = geometry.client_wants_fullscreen(client)
        if wants and workspace.fullscreen is not client:
            geometry.set_fullscreen(server, workspace, client)
        elif not wants and workspace.fullscreen is client:
            geometry.set_fullscreen(server, workspace, None)
        geometry.apply_tree(server)
        geometry.reconcile(server, monitor)
        focus.reconcile(server)


def on_request_maximize(server: Server, client: Client, _data) -> None:
    """An app asked to (un)maximize. We don't maximize, but xdg-shell still
    requires a configure in reply, so ack the request with an empty one."""
    # Clients may request maximize before their first commit; scheduling a
    # configure then trips a wlroots assertion. The initial configure covers it.
    if client.toplevel.base.initialized:
        server.lib.wlr_xdg_surface_schedule_configure(client.toplevel.base)


def on_request_activate(server: Server, data) -> None:
    """An app asked to be brought to the foreground. We never steal focus:
    the window just shows an urgent border until the user focuses it."""
    event = server.ffi.cast(
        "struct wlr_xdg_activation_v1_request_activate_event *", data)
    client = focus.client_for_surface(server, event.surface)
    if client is not None:
        mark_urgent(server, client)


def mark_urgent(server: Server, client: Client) -> None:
    """Flag a window as wanting attention: it shows an urgent border until the
    user focuses it. No-op if it already has focus."""
    focused = focus.client_for_surface(
        server, server.seat.keyboard_state.focused_surface)
    if client is focused:
        return
    client.urgent = True
    geometry.set_border_color(server, client, model.BORDER_COLOR_URGENT)
    if server.ext_workspace is not None:
        ext_workspace.publish(server)


def on_destroy(_server: Server, client: Client, _data) -> None:
    """Fires when an app closes a window (or its connection drops). The
    visible work happened in unmap (if the window was ever mapped); this
    just detaches listeners."""
    for listener in client.listeners:
        listener.remove()
    client.listeners.clear()


def create_scene(server: Server, client: Client) -> None:
    """Build the window's wrapper scene tree: the app's content subtree inset
    within four border rects. The inset is reapplied per-resize so fullscreen
    can collapse it to 0."""
    ffi, lib = server.ffi, server.lib
    client.scene_tree = lib.wlr_scene_tree_create(server.layers[Layer.TILE])
    if isinstance(client, X11Client):
        client.content_tree = lib.wlr_scene_subsurface_tree_create(
            client.scene_tree, client.xsurface.surface)
    else:
        client.content_tree = lib.wlr_scene_xdg_surface_create(
            client.scene_tree, client.toplevel.base)
        # Anchor for popups: wlr_xdg_popup.parent points at this wlr_surface,
        # and popup_new reads .data to find the parent scene tree.
        client.toplevel.base.surface.data = ffi.cast(
            "void *", client.scene_tree)
    color = ffi.new("float[4]", model.BORDER_COLOR_INACTIVE)
    client.borders = tuple(
        lib.wlr_scene_rect_create(client.scene_tree, 0, 0, color)
        for _ in range(4))


def popup_new(server: Server, data) -> None:
    """Fires when an app creates a transient sub-window (menu, tooltip,
    autocomplete). The scene node is deferred to the popup's first commit
    so the parent surface is fully set up first."""
    ffi, lib, listen = server.ffi, server.lib, server.listen
    popup = ffi.cast("struct wlr_xdg_popup *", data)

    def on_popup_commit(_data):
        if popup.base.initial_commit:
            # Short-circuit guards popup.parent.data when popup.parent is NULL.
            parent_scene = (
                ffi.cast("struct wlr_scene_tree *", popup.parent.data)
                if popup.parent != ffi.NULL  # pylint: disable=consider-using-in
                    and popup.parent.data != ffi.NULL
                else None
            )
            if parent_scene is not None:
                scene = lib.wlr_scene_xdg_surface_create(
                    parent_scene, popup.base)
                popup.base.surface.data = ffi.cast("void *", scene)
                root = lib.wlr_surface_get_root_surface(popup.parent)
                owner_node, owner_monitor = _popup_owner(server, root)
                if owner_monitor is not None and owner_node is not None:
                    box = geometry.monitor_box(server, owner_monitor)
                    wlr_box = ffi.new("struct wlr_box *",
                        [box.x - owner_node.x, box.y - owner_node.y,
                         box.width, box.height])
                    lib.wlr_xdg_popup_unconstrain_from_box(popup, wlr_box)
            cleanup()

    def cleanup():
        for l in listeners:
            l.remove()
        listeners.clear()

    listeners = [
        listen(lib.welpy_surface_commit(popup.base.surface), on_popup_commit),
        listen(lib.welpy_xdg_popup_destroy(popup), lambda _data: cleanup()),
    ]


def _popup_owner(server: Server, root_surface):
    """The (parent-scene-node, monitor) for the window owning this surface,
    or (None, None) if no window claims it."""
    for c in server.clients:
        if (c.scene_tree is not None
                and geometry.client_surface(c) == root_surface):
            return c.scene_tree.node, model.client_monitor(c)
    for m in server.monitors:
        for bucket in m.layers.values():
            for ls in bucket:
                if ls.layer_surface.surface == root_surface:
                    return ls.scene_tree.node, ls.monitor
    return None, None

"""XWayland integration for legacy X11 apps: managed windows,
override-redirect surfaces, activation/configure requests, and startup
readiness."""

from __future__ import annotations

from . import focus
from . import geometry
from . import windows
from .model import Layer, Server, Unmanaged, X11Client


def on_create(server: Server, data) -> None:
    """Fires when an X11 app creates a window. Managed windows get the same
    lifecycle wiring as Wayland ones; override-redirect surfaces (menus,
    tooltips, drag icons) take the lighter unmanaged path."""
    ffi, lib, listen = server.ffi, server.lib, server.listen
    xsurface = ffi.cast("struct wlr_xwayland_surface *", data)
    if xsurface.override_redirect:
        unmanaged_new(server, xsurface)
        return
    client = X11Client(
        xsurface=xsurface, scene_tree=None, content_tree=None, borders=(),
        focus_order=0, urgent=False, grab=None, floating_geom=None,
        workspace=None, listeners=[], decoration=None, handle=None,
        inner_size=None)

    # The wl_surface only exists between associate and dissociate, so its
    # map/unmap/commit listeners are wired on associate and dropped after.
    surface_listeners = []

    def on_associate(_data):
        surface = xsurface.surface
        surface_listeners.extend([
            listen(lib.welpy_surface_map(surface),
                lambda data: windows.on_map(server, client, data)),
            listen(lib.welpy_surface_unmap(surface),
                lambda data: windows.on_unmap(server, client, data)),
            listen(lib.welpy_surface_commit(surface),
                lambda data: windows.on_commit(server, client, data)),
        ])

    def on_dissociate(_data):
        for l in surface_listeners:
            l.remove()
        surface_listeners.clear()

    client.listeners.extend([
        listen(lib.welpy_xwayland_surface_associate(xsurface), on_associate),
        listen(lib.welpy_xwayland_surface_dissociate(xsurface), on_dissociate),
        listen(lib.welpy_xwayland_surface_destroy(xsurface),
            lambda data: windows.on_destroy(server, client, data)),
        listen(lib.welpy_xwayland_surface_request_configure(xsurface),
            lambda data: on_request_configure(server, client, data)),
        listen(lib.welpy_xwayland_surface_request_fullscreen(xsurface),
            lambda data: windows.on_request_fullscreen(
                server, client, data)),
        listen(lib.welpy_xwayland_surface_request_activate(xsurface),
            lambda _data: on_request_activate(server, client)),
        listen(lib.welpy_xwayland_surface_set_hints(xsurface),
            lambda _data: on_set_hints(server, client)),
    ])


def on_request_configure(server: Server, client: X11Client, data) -> None:
    """An X11 app asked for a position/size. Honor it before the window maps
    (its initial geometry); once mapped the layout owns geometry, so reassert
    ours."""
    ffi, lib = server.ffi, server.lib
    event = ffi.cast("struct wlr_xwayland_surface_configure_event *", data)
    if client.scene_tree is None:
        lib.wlr_xwayland_surface_configure(
            client.xsurface, event.x, event.y, event.width, event.height)
    elif client.inner_size is not None:
        geometry.configure_x11(server, client, *client.inner_size)


def on_request_activate(server: Server, client: X11Client) -> None:
    """An X11 app asked for the foreground; show an urgent border instead of
    stealing focus."""
    if client.scene_tree is not None:
        windows.mark_urgent(server, client)


def on_set_hints(server: Server, client: X11Client) -> None:
    """An X11 app updated its ICCCM hints; show an urgent border if it set the
    urgency flag while unfocused."""
    if (client.scene_tree is not None
            and server.lib.welpy_xwayland_surface_is_urgent(client.xsurface)):
        windows.mark_urgent(server, client)


def on_ready(server: Server) -> None:
    """Fires once the embedded X server is up. Point it at our seat and give it
    a default cursor."""
    lib = server.lib
    lib.wlr_xwayland_set_seat(server.xwayland, server.seat)
    lib.welpy_xwayland_set_default_cursor(
        server.xwayland, server.cursor.xcursor_manager)


def unmanaged_new(server: Server, xsurface) -> None:
    """Fires for an override-redirect X11 surface (menu, tooltip, dropdown).
    Wires its map/unmap lifecycle; we don't manage it as a window."""
    lib, listen = server.lib, server.listen
    um = Unmanaged(xsurface=xsurface, scene_tree=None, listeners=[])

    # The wl_surface only exists between associate and dissociate.
    surface_listeners = []

    def on_associate(_data):
        surface = xsurface.surface
        surface_listeners.extend([
            listen(lib.welpy_surface_map(surface),
                lambda data: unmanaged_map(server, um, data)),
            listen(lib.welpy_surface_unmap(surface),
                lambda data: unmanaged_unmap(server, um, data)),
        ])

    def on_dissociate(_data):
        for l in surface_listeners:
            l.remove()
        surface_listeners.clear()

    um.listeners.extend([
        listen(lib.welpy_xwayland_surface_associate(xsurface), on_associate),
        listen(lib.welpy_xwayland_surface_dissociate(xsurface), on_dissociate),
        listen(lib.welpy_xwayland_surface_destroy(xsurface),
            lambda data: unmanaged_cleanup(server, um, data)),
        listen(lib.welpy_xwayland_surface_request_configure(xsurface),
            lambda data: unmanaged_configure(server, um, data)),
    ])


def unmanaged_map(server: Server, um: Unmanaged, _data) -> None:
    """Place an override-redirect surface at the coords the app requested,
    above the windows, and give it the keyboard if it wants it."""
    ffi, lib = server.ffi, server.lib
    xsurface = um.xsurface
    um.scene_tree = lib.wlr_scene_subsurface_tree_create(
        server.layers[Layer.OVERLAY], xsurface.surface)
    lib.wlr_scene_node_set_position(
        ffi.addressof(um.scene_tree.node), xsurface.x, xsurface.y)
    lib.wlr_scene_node_raise_to_top(ffi.addressof(um.scene_tree.node))
    if lib.wlr_xwayland_surface_override_redirect_wants_focus(xsurface):
        server.unmanaged_focus = um
        focus.reconcile(server)


def unmanaged_configure(server: Server, um: Unmanaged, data) -> None:
    """An override-redirect surface asked to move/resize itself; honor it and
    keep its scene node in sync."""
    ffi, lib = server.ffi, server.lib
    event = ffi.cast("struct wlr_xwayland_surface_configure_event *", data)
    lib.wlr_xwayland_surface_configure(
        um.xsurface, event.x, event.y, event.width, event.height)
    if um.scene_tree is not None:
        lib.wlr_scene_node_set_position(
            ffi.addressof(um.scene_tree.node), event.x, event.y)


def unmanaged_unmap(server: Server, um: Unmanaged, _data) -> None:
    """Tear down an override-redirect surface's scene node and return the
    keyboard to whoever had it before."""
    ffi, lib = server.ffi, server.lib
    if um.scene_tree is not None:
        lib.wlr_scene_node_destroy(ffi.addressof(um.scene_tree.node))
        um.scene_tree = None
    if server.unmanaged_focus is um:
        server.unmanaged_focus = None
        focus.reconcile(server)


def unmanaged_cleanup(server: Server, um: Unmanaged, _data) -> None:
    """Fires when an override-redirect surface is destroyed; drop listeners."""
    if server.unmanaged_focus is um:
        server.unmanaged_focus = None
    for listener in um.listeners:
        listener.remove()
    um.listeners.clear()

"""Layer-shell surface lifecycle for bars, wallpaper, panels, and launchers."""

from __future__ import annotations

from . import focus
from . import geometry
from . import model
from .model import Layer, LayerSurface, Server


def on_create(server: Server, data) -> None:
    """Fires when an app creates a shell-anchored window (bar, wallpaper,
    launcher). Sets up its scene tree; real geometry lands at first commit."""
    ffi, lib, listen = server.ffi, server.lib, server.listen
    layer_surface = ffi.cast("struct wlr_layer_surface_v1 *", data)
    if layer_surface.output == ffi.NULL:
        monitor = server.active_monitor
        if monitor is not None:
            layer_surface.output = monitor.output
    else:
        monitor = next(
            (m for m in server.monitors if m.output == layer_surface.output),
            None)

    if monitor is None:
        lib.wlr_layer_surface_v1_destroy(layer_surface)
    else:
        layer = model.SHELL_LAYERS[layer_surface.pending.layer]
        scene_layer = lib.wlr_scene_layer_surface_v1_create(
            server.layers[layer], layer_surface)
        # Lift popups for BG/BOTTOM into TOP so they aren't buried by a bar.
        popups_parent = server.layers[
            Layer.TOP if layer in (Layer.BACKGROUND, Layer.BOTTOM) else layer]
        popups_tree = lib.wlr_scene_tree_create(popups_parent)
        ls = LayerSurface(
            layer_surface=layer_surface, scene_layer=scene_layer,
            scene_tree=scene_layer.tree, popups_tree=popups_tree,
            monitor=monitor, focused=False, mapped=False, listeners=[])
        # popup_new resolves the parent scene tree via surface.data.
        layer_surface.surface.data = ffi.cast("void *", popups_tree)
        monitor.layers[layer].append(ls)
        ls.listeners.extend([
            listen(lib.welpy_surface_commit(layer_surface.surface),
                lambda data: on_commit(server, ls, data)),
            listen(lib.welpy_surface_unmap(layer_surface.surface),
                lambda data: on_unmap(server, ls, data)),
            listen(lib.welpy_layer_surface_destroy(layer_surface),
                lambda data: on_destroy(server, ls, data)),
        ])
        lib.wlr_surface_send_enter(layer_surface.surface, monitor.output)


def on_commit(server: Server, ls: LayerSurface, _data) -> None:
    """Fires every time the shell surface commits new state."""
    ffi = server.ffi
    layer_surface = ls.layer_surface
    monitor = ls.monitor
    if monitor is not None:
        if layer_surface.initial_commit:
            # Swap pending into current so the initial configure sees real size.
            size = ffi.sizeof("struct wlr_layer_surface_v1_state")
            saved = ffi.new("struct wlr_layer_surface_v1_state *")
            ffi.memmove(saved, ffi.addressof(layer_surface, "current"), size)
            ffi.memmove(ffi.addressof(layer_surface, "current"),
                        ffi.addressof(layer_surface, "pending"), size)
            geometry.arrange_layers(server, monitor)
            ffi.memmove(ffi.addressof(layer_surface, "current"), saved, size)
        else:
            # Re-arrange sends a configure the client acks with a commit, so a
            # plain content commit (clock tick) would loop unless state changed.
            surface = layer_surface.surface
            changed = (
                layer_surface.current.committed != 0
                or ls.mapped != surface.mapped
            )
            if changed:
                ls.mapped = surface.mapped
                geometry.place_in_layer_bucket(
                    monitor, ls,
                    model.SHELL_LAYERS[layer_surface.current.layer])
                geometry.arrange_layers(server, monitor)
                geometry.apply_tree(server)
                geometry.reconcile(server, monitor)
                focus.reconcile(server)


def on_unmap(server: Server, ls: LayerSurface, _data) -> None:
    """Fires when the shell surface stops showing; reclaims its space."""
    was_focused = ls.focused
    ls.focused = False
    if ls.monitor is not None:
        geometry.arrange_layers(server, ls.monitor)
    if was_focused:
        top = focus.top_client(server, ls.monitor)
        if top is not None:
            focus.bump_focus_order(server, top)
    if ls.monitor is not None:
        geometry.reconcile(server, ls.monitor)
    focus.reconcile(server)


def on_destroy(
        server: Server, ls: LayerSurface, _data) -> None:
    """Fires when a shell surface is destroyed (app close, output gone)."""
    ffi, lib = server.ffi, server.lib
    for listener in ls.listeners:
        listener.remove()
    ls.listeners.clear()
    if ls.monitor is not None:
        for bucket in ls.monitor.layers.values():
            if ls in bucket:
                bucket.remove(ls)
                break
        ls.monitor = None
    # wlr_scene_layer_surface_v1's destroy listener fires before ours
    # and frees scene_tree.
    lib.wlr_scene_node_destroy(ffi.addressof(ls.popups_tree.node))

"""Screen-lock (session lock) support: blank every screen and hand a locker
app the top of the scene until it authenticates the user."""

from __future__ import annotations

import logging

from . import focus
from . import geometry
from .model import Layer, LockSurface, Server, SessionLock

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


def on_lock(server: Server, data) -> None:
    """A screen-locker app asked to lock the screen. Blank every screen and
    hand the locker the top of the scene until it authenticates the user."""
    ffi, lib, listen = server.ffi, server.lib, server.listen
    lock = ffi.cast("struct wlr_session_lock_v1 *", data)
    lib.wlr_scene_node_set_enabled(
        lib.welpy_scene_rect_node(server.lock_background), True)
    lib.wlr_seat_pointer_clear_focus(server.seat)
    for client in server.clients:
        client.grab = None
    if server.session_lock is not None:
        # Only one locker at a time; reject the latecomer.
        lib.wlr_session_lock_v1_destroy(lock)
        return
    tree = lib.wlr_scene_tree_create(server.layers[Layer.LOCK])
    session_lock = SessionLock(
        lock=lock, tree=tree, surfaces=[], listeners=[])
    server.session_lock = session_lock
    server.locked = True
    session_lock.listeners.extend([
        listen(lib.welpy_session_lock_new_surface(lock),
            lambda data: on_surface_created(server, data)),
        listen(lib.welpy_session_lock_unlock(lock),
            lambda _data: on_unlock(server)),
        listen(lib.welpy_session_lock_destroy(lock),
            lambda _data: on_destroy(server)),
    ])
    lib.wlr_session_lock_v1_send_locked(lock)
    focus.reconcile(server)


def on_surface_created(server: Server, data) -> None:
    """The locker created its blanking surface for one screen."""
    ffi, lib, listen = server.ffi, server.lib, server.listen
    lock_surface = ffi.cast("struct wlr_session_lock_surface_v1 *", data)
    monitor = next(
        (m for m in server.monitors if m.output == lock_surface.output), None)
    if server.session_lock is None:
        return
    if monitor is None:
        logger.warning("ignoring lock surface for unknown screen")
        return
    scene_tree = lib.wlr_scene_subsurface_tree_create(
        server.session_lock.tree, lock_surface.surface)
    box = geometry.monitor_box(server, monitor)
    lib.wlr_scene_node_set_position(
        ffi.addressof(scene_tree.node), box.x, box.y)
    lib.wlr_session_lock_surface_v1_configure(
        lock_surface, box.width, box.height)
    ls = LockSurface(
        lock_surface=lock_surface, monitor=monitor, scene_tree=scene_tree,
        listeners=[])
    server.session_lock.surfaces.append(ls)
    ls.listeners.append(
        listen(lib.welpy_session_lock_surface_destroy(lock_surface),
            lambda _data: on_surface_destroyed(server, ls)))
    focus.reconcile(server)


def on_surface_destroyed(server: Server, ls: LockSurface) -> None:
    """A lock surface went away; drop it and move focus to a sibling."""
    for listener in ls.listeners:
        listener.remove()
    ls.listeners.clear()
    if server.session_lock is not None and ls in server.session_lock.surfaces:
        server.session_lock.surfaces.remove(ls)
    focus.reconcile(server)


def on_unlock(server: Server) -> None:
    """The locker authenticated the user; reveal the screen again."""
    teardown(server, unlocked=True)


def on_destroy(server: Server) -> None:
    """The locker vanished without unlocking (e.g. it crashed). Stay locked
    with a blank screen so window contents aren't exposed."""
    teardown(server, unlocked=False)


def teardown(server: Server, unlocked: bool) -> None:
    """Tear down the active lock. `unlocked` reveals the screen; otherwise the
    blanking rectangle stays up so a crashed locker can't leak contents."""
    ffi, lib = server.ffi, server.lib
    session_lock = server.session_lock
    if session_lock is None:
        return
    for ls in session_lock.surfaces:
        for listener in ls.listeners:
            listener.remove()
        ls.listeners.clear()
    session_lock.surfaces.clear()
    for listener in session_lock.listeners:
        listener.remove()
    session_lock.listeners.clear()
    lib.wlr_scene_node_destroy(ffi.addressof(session_lock.tree.node))
    server.session_lock = None
    if unlocked:
        server.locked = False
        lib.wlr_scene_node_set_enabled(
            lib.welpy_scene_rect_node(server.lock_background), False)
    focus.reconcile(server)


def update_background(server: Server) -> None:
    """Size the blanking rectangle to cover the whole screen layout."""
    ffi, lib = server.ffi, server.lib
    box = ffi.new("struct wlr_box *")
    lib.wlr_output_layout_get_box(server.output_layout, ffi.NULL, box)
    lib.wlr_scene_node_set_position(
        lib.welpy_scene_rect_node(server.lock_background), box.x, box.y)
    lib.wlr_scene_rect_set_size(
        server.lock_background, box.width, box.height)


def update_surfaces(server: Server) -> None:
    """Keep active lock surfaces matched to their current screens."""
    ffi, lib = server.ffi, server.lib
    if server.session_lock is None:
        return
    for ls in list(server.session_lock.surfaces):
        if ls.monitor not in server.monitors:
            on_surface_destroyed(server, ls)
        else:
            box = geometry.monitor_box(server, ls.monitor)
            lib.wlr_scene_node_set_position(
                ffi.addressof(ls.scene_tree.node), box.x, box.y)
            lib.wlr_session_lock_surface_v1_configure(
                ls.lock_surface, box.width, box.height)


def create_background(ffi, lib, tree):
    """Black rectangle kept on top of every window while the screen is locked;
    sized to the whole layout by update_background."""
    black = ffi.new("float[4]", (0.0, 0.0, 0.0, 1.0))
    rect = lib.wlr_scene_rect_create(tree, 0, 0, black)
    lib.wlr_scene_node_set_enabled(lib.welpy_scene_rect_node(rect), False)
    return rect

def set_size(
        server: Server, client: Client, width: int, height: int) -> None:
    """Tell this window what size to render at."""
    if isinstance(client, X11Client):
        configure_x11(server, client, width, height)
        return
    if client.inner_size == (width, height):
        return
    serial = server.lib.wlr_xdg_toplevel_set_size(
        client.toplevel, width, height)
    _track_configure(client, serial)


def set_tiled(server: Server, client: Client, edges: int) -> None:
    """Tell this window which of its edges are flush against neighbors or
    screen borders, so the app can suppress decorations on those edges."""
    if isinstance(client, X11Client):
        return  # X11 has no tiled-edge state.
    serial = server.lib.wlr_xdg_toplevel_set_tiled(client.toplevel, edges)
    _track_configure(client, serial)


def set_fullscreen(
        server: Server, workspace, client: Client | None) -> None:
    """Set this workspace's fullscreen window (None to clear), notifying the
    affected apps so their window state matches."""
    def _fullscreen(target: Client, fullscreen: bool) -> None:
        if isinstance(target, X11Client):
            server.lib.wlr_xwayland_surface_set_fullscreen(
                target.xsurface, fullscreen)
            return
        serial = server.lib.wlr_xdg_toplevel_set_fullscreen(
            target.toplevel, fullscreen)
        _track_configure(target, serial)

    prev = workspace.fullscreen
    if prev is not client:
        workspace.fullscreen = client
        if prev is not None:
            _fullscreen(prev, False)
        if client is not None:
            _fullscreen(client, True)


def set_border_color(server: Server, client: Client, color) -> None:
    """Paint every edge of this window's border with the same RGBA color."""
    color_arr = server.ffi.new("float[4]", color)
    for rect in client.borders:
        server.lib.wlr_scene_rect_set_color(rect, color_arr)


def set_activated(server: Server, client: Client, activated: bool) -> None:
    """Tell this window whether it has focus, so the app can render its
    focused state (title-bar styling, cursor blink, etc.)."""
    if isinstance(client, X11Client):
        server.lib.wlr_xwayland_surface_activate(client.xsurface, activated)
        return
    server.lib.wlr_xdg_toplevel_set_activated(client.toplevel, activated)


def configure_x11(
        server: Server, client: X11Client, width: int, height: int) -> None:
    """Configure an X11 window. X11 couples position and size in one request,
    so derive the absolute content origin from the already-placed wrapper."""
    bw = 0 if client_layer(client) == Layer.FULLSCREEN else model.BORDER_WIDTH
    node = client.scene_tree.node
    x, y = node.x + bw, node.y + bw
    xs = client.xsurface
    # wlroots re-sends a ConfigureNotify even when the geometry is unchanged.
    if (xs.x, xs.y, xs.width, xs.height) != (x, y, width, height):
        server.lib.wlr_xwayland_surface_configure(xs, x, y, width, height)


def _track_configure(client: Client, serial: int) -> None:
    """Remember the configure we sent so we can hold the screen until the
    client renders at the new state."""
    acked = client.toplevel.base.current.configure_serial

        # branch shouldn't be reachable; clear pending defensively.

            "configure serial %d already acked (current=%d)", serial, acked)
        client.pending_serial = None
    else:
        client.pending_serial = serial

class Client: # pylint: disable=too-many-instance-attributes
    """Application window. Base shared by xdg-shell and X11/XWayland windows;
    per-kind fields live on the subclasses below."""
    scene_tree: Any          # wrapper tree: content subtree + four border rects
    content_tree: Any        # content subtree; inset within the wrapper
    borders: tuple           # (top, bottom, left, right) wlr_scene_rect handles
    focus_order: int         # bumped on each focus; higher = more recent
    urgent: bool             # app asked for attention while unfocused
    grab: Grab | None        # active mouse drag (move/resize), or None
    floating_geom: Rect | None  # the float's rect; None means tiled
    workspace: Any           # Workspace this window belongs to; None pre-map
    listeners: list[Any]
    decoration: Any          # wlr_xdg_toplevel_decoration_v1, if any; X11: None
    handle: Any              # ffi.new_handle: backs the surface's back-pointer
    inner_size: tuple[int, int] | None  # inner (w, h) configured; None pre-map


@dataclass(kw_only=True)
class XdgClient(Client):
    """A Wayland-native window speaking the xdg-shell protocol."""
    toplevel: Any            # the window (xdg_toplevel role on a wl_surface)
    pending_serial: int | None   # configure serial; None when client caught up


@dataclass(kw_only=True)
class X11Client(Client):
    """A legacy X11 window bridged through the embedded XWayland server."""
    xsurface: Any            # wlr_xwayland_surface


@dataclass(kw_only=True)
class Unmanaged:
    """An override-redirect X11 surface (menu, tooltip, dropdown, drag icon):
    the app positions and stacks it itself, so we just place it above the
    windows and hand it the keyboard when it asks."""
    xsurface: Any            # wlr_xwayland_surface
    scene_tree: Any          # subsurface tree in OVERLAY layer; None pre-map
    listeners: list[Any]


@dataclass
class SessionLock:
    """An active screen lock: a locker app has taken over every screen and
    holds it until it authenticates the user (or crashes)."""
    lock: Any                # wlr_session_lock_v1

    """Forget IME popup scene nodes parented under a dying window tree."""
    text_input.drop_popups_for_scene_tree(server, scene_tree)


def ime_window_anchor(server: Server, surface):
